name: Peter Piper's Pickled Pumpkin
recipeIngredient: 2 chunks Fresh ginger root (or 1/4 cup Chopped crystallized ginger)
6 oz Frozen orange juice concentrate; thawed
recipeInstructions:
Or you may use 2 or 3 cups Sugar and 2 cups Honey.
The recipe makes eight to nine 1 pint jars. You will need the basic equipment for boiling water bath canning.
1. Organize and prepare ingredients, equipment, and work space.
2. Wash the pumpkin, cut it into 1 inch chunks, and peel. You should have about 4 quarts of chunks.
3. In a preserving kettle, combine sugar (or sugar honey combination), vinegar, water, and spices. Tie spices in cheesecloth, if desired.
4. Heat over high heat until boiling, STIRRING CONSTANTLY.
5. Continue to heat and stir until the sugar dissolves.
6. Stir in the pumpkin chunks and orange juice concentrate and heat to boiling.
7. Reduce heat to simmer and cook, stirring occasionally, until pumpkin is just barely tender, about 30 minutes.
8. Ladle into hot jars leaving 1/4 inch of headspace, spooning in the hot liquid from kettle. Run a slim, non-metal tool along the insides of jars to release any air bubbles. Add additional hot liquid to jars if necessary to reach 1/4 inch headspace.
9. Wipe tops and threads of jars with a damp clean cloth.
10. Place lids and screw bands in place as manufacturer directs.
11. Process in boiling water bath for 10 minutes. Follow basic steps for boiling water bath canning, 10 through 18.
